Galilee does not show the bilingual ecology reflected in Mark
chapter_5.section: Why the Evidence Shows Jesus Did Not Speak Aramaic (lines 1291-1303)

chapter_5.argument: Once each gospel is placed in its proper regional environment, long-standing mysteries across all four texts resolve themselves.
evidence: Matthew: prophecy obsession, fulfillment quotation density, double genealogy through Joseph and Mary = Judean elite dynastic practice; Sermon on the Mount = Judean halakhic discourse in Greek; anti-Pharisee polemic = intra-Judean elite conflict
Luke: census narrative integrates Jesus into bureaucratic categories; tight chronological framework with named officials; interest in Roman officials; universalism matches Aegean polis worldview

John: Logos = Alexandrian Jewish philosophy; long discourses = Alexandrian philosophical dialogues; light/darkness dualisms = Alexandrian Platonized Jewish worldview; high Christology from Alexandrian Judaism
Mark: exorcism focus matches Syrian religious culture; abrupt scene shifts and heroic structure fit Syrian literary taste for Greek popular epic

chapter_5.argument: The epistolary map shows each epistle addresses a region matching one of the four imperial poles, with earliest attestation and papyrus evidence supporting the clustering.
evidence: Romans, 1-2 Corinthians, Galatians, Ephesians/Colossians, Philippians, 1-2 Thessalonians, Philemon: Aegean/Pauline pole; attested by 1 Clement, Ignatius, Polycarp; P46
Hebrews: Alexandrian homily; discussed by Clement/Origen; P13, P46
James, Jude: Judea/Jerusalem pole; attested by Origen, Irenaeus
1-2 Peter: Seleucid provinces; attested by Polycarp, Irenaeus; P72
1-3 John: Egyptian pole; attested by Irenaeus, Muratorian Fragment; P9
Marcion (c. 140): Aegean canon (edited Luke + Pauline Apostolikon)
Egypt: strongest early Johannine cluster in papyrus caches

chapter_5.argument: Revelation functions as an operational war text and imperial restoration document, not merely apocalyptic consolation.
evidence: Beast = Nero (666 = Hebrew gematria of Neron Kaisar; 616 = Latin variant)
Babylon = Rome (city on seven hills, rules over kings of the earth; Rev 17:9, 17:18)
Four living creatures around God's throne (Rev 4:6-8): legitimate imperial plurality with differentiated powers
Babylon's beast: single hybrid monster absorbing lion, bear, leopard (Rev 13:2) = Rome's totalizing empire
Dragon (Rev 12-13) distinct from Beast: 'gave the Beast his power and his throne' (Rev 13:2) = supra-political principle of universal empire
Dragon represents ideology of Hellenistic divine kingship passing from Persia to Alexander to Diadochi to Rome
Christ appears as basileus with diadems commanding heavenly strateuma
'He shall shepherd the nations with a rod of iron' — sovereignty/governance vocabulary
chapter_5.section: Revelation as Imperial Restoration by Revolt from Rome (lines 1388-1423)

chapter_5.argument: The seven messages of Revelation 2-3 function as assessments of readiness along the primary Roman communication circuit of Asia province.
evidence: Sequence follows Roman communication circuit: Ephesus (provincial capital) → Smyrna → Pergamum → Thyatira → Sardis → Philadelphia → Laodicea
Route corresponds to main postal, military, commercial axis used by Persian, Hellenistic, and Roman administrations

Each city occupies strategic position: provincial capital (Ephesus), imperial cult center (Smyrna), former Attalid capital (Pergamum), inland garrison (Thyatira), Lydian admin center (Sardis), frontier settlement (Philadelphia), financial hub (Laodicea)
Messages follow inspection report form: knowledge of works, commendation/deficiency, command to correct, consequences for noncompliance
Threats as termination clauses: removal of lampstand, surprise visitation, expulsion
'The one who conquers' (ho nikon) frames sequence in terms of victory/imperial contest
Contrast with Paul: Pauline networks expand across provinces; Johannine circuit is closed loop within single administrative unit (audit vs. mission)

chapter_5.argument: Revelation's seals, trumpets, and bowls form a structured operational sequence from inspection to execution of regime change.
evidence: Seals: establish legal authority over succession and judgment, initiate destabilization (famine, violence, death)
Trumpets: mobilization signals and shock operations targeting infrastructure, population, cosmic legitimacy
Bowls: terminal judgments incapacitating regime's capacity to govern and enforce loyalty
Four horsemen = initial destabilization phase
Two witnesses = juridical envoys asserting rival authority; execution and vindication signal loss of mandate
Mark of the Beast (charagma): economic control encoding allegiance to Rome's cultic state
Seal of God (sphragis): counter-administrative system marking subjects of rival sovereignty
Woman clothed with the sun = dynastic continuity; child's removal to God's throne preserves succession
Rider on white horse: sovereign executing judgment with multiple diadems; name Logos tou Theou
New Jerusalem: rival capital with walls, gates, foundations, throne — displaces Rome's urban theology
Kings bringing glory into city presupposes political reorganization, not abolition of history
